title: "Open Office Issue"
date: 2010-02-24
forum: General Help

### Post by TheFloridiot on 2010-02-24
Over the past few weeks, I'd been writing a paper on Open Office, saving frequently and whatnot. Just last night, I'd saved it and told my computer to shut down. It started going through the process, so I closed the lid and walked away. I came back a few minutes later to see the laptop still on, but nothing displaying on the screen (the screen itself was off, but all the lights were on, as was the mouselight.) I hit buttons and clicked, trying to get the monitor to come back on, even tried the keyboard shortcut but nothing happened. I held down the power save button, turning it off, and when I went back to it this morning, the icon had been changed to an ordinary .doc icon, and the contents were 

```
"
ADD 3825205442 "def" "/apps/avant-window-navigator" "IOR:"
ADD 3808428225 "def" "/apps/avant-window-navigator" "IOR:"
ADD 3791651008 "def" "/apps/avant-window-navigator" "IOR:"
ADD 3758096574 "def" "/apps/avant-window-navigator" "IOR:"
ADD 3942645951 "def" "/apps" "IOR:"
ADD 2768240773 "def" "/apps/bluetooth-manager" "IOR:"
ADD 3724541983 "def" "/apps/brasero" "IOR:"
ADD 2717909090 "def" "/apps/compiz/general/screen0/options" "IOR:"
ADD 3590324405 "def" "/apps/compiz" "IOR:"
ADD 3707764924 "def" "/apps/compiz/plugins/decoration/allscreens/options" "IOR:"
ADD 671088731 "def" "/apps/evolution/addressbook/sources" "IOR:"
ADD 4160749587 "def" "/apps/evolution/addressbook/sources" "IOR:"
ADD 4043309111 "def" "/apps/evolution/calendar/sources" "IOR:"
ADD 4143972419 "def" "/apps/evolution/mail/accounts" "IOR:"
ADD 4177526811 "def" "/apps/evolution/memos/sources" "IOR:"
ADD 4076863489 "def" "/apps/evolution/shell" "IOR:"
ADD 4060086292 "def" "/apps/evolution/tasks/sources" "IOR:"
ADD 2919235726 "def" "/apps/gnome-power-manager" "IOR:"
ADD 637534261 "def" "/apps/gnome-settings/Gecko" "IOR:"
ADD 4026531898 "def" "/apps/gnome-settings/evolution-alarm-notify" "IOR:"
ADD 4127195228 "def" "/apps/gnome-settings/evolution-exchange" "IOR:"
ADD 2701131787 "def" "/apps/gnome-settings/gnome-panel" "IOR:"
ADD 2449473654 "def" "/apps/gnome_settings_daemon/gtk-modules" "IOR:"
ADD 2650800258 "def" "/apps/gnome_settings_daemon/keybindings" "IOR:"
ADD 2315255918 "def" "/apps/gnome_settings_daemon/plugins" "IOR:"
ADD 2332033135 "def" "/apps/gnome_settings_daemon/xrandr" "IOR:"
ADD 3657433273 "def" "/apps/gwd" "IOR:"
ADD 3674210490 "def" "/apps/metacity/general" "IOR:"
ADD 2902458509 "def" "/apps/metacity/general" "IOR:
```

I have no idea what happened, and I tried looking in the backup folders for it to no avail. I assume the problem was me turning it off, even though I'd saved it. But any idea what happened exactly, and is there anything I can do?

---

### Post by mechro on 2010-02-24
Odd... most of that looks like a **.gconfd/saved_state** file but I have no idea what that means.

### Post by mechro on 2010-02-24
There's a chance that the file has become "deleted" and you may be able to recover it using **Testdisk** or **Photorec**. Testdisk is generally used for recovering partition tables but it can also undelete files as can Photorec.

If available, boot a LiveCD and install Testdisk via synaptic (Photorec is installed as part of the Testdisk package). Start Testdisk with **sudo testdisk**

[http://www.cgsecurity.org/wiki/TestDisk](http://www.cgsecurity.org/wiki/TestDisk)

---

### Post by ndefontenay on 2010-02-24
Did you try to open read-only then IF it can iopen it save it again with a different name?

---

### Post by Hagar Delest on 2010-02-25
Have you checked the OOo backup folder? See [[Tutorial] The OOo user profile](http://user.services.openoffice.org/en/forum/viewtopic.php?f=74&t=12426).

The file doesn't look like a .doc (that is unreadable BTW, you would have got strange characters only).

Basically, you should work in native ODF and export when needed only.

---

### Post by mechro on 2010-02-27
> **Hagar de l'Est said:**
> A .doc file is not human readable. My 'that' is related to a .doc file, not the file shown here.

Sorry, the point I was making is that the OP's file now looks like a **.gconfd/saved_state** file so his/her doc has been replaced with a gnome system file.

+1 for your suggestion of checking the OOo backup folder.

I've also suggested Testdisk/Photorec but it looks like the OP is busy rewriting all the hard work... poor soul! ;)

---

### Post by Hagar Delest on 2010-02-27
> **mechro said:**
> Sorry, the point I was making is that the OP's file now looks like a **.gconfd/saved_state** file so his/her doc has been replaced with a gnome system file.
Yes, I know. My point is that as soon as you can read something in the file, then it can't be a .doc file.

+1 for Photorec, tried it successfully in the past (even gave some bucks to the author for bringing back to life lost pictures!).

---
